description: Eight worked eval scenarios (A–H): web research and durable memory (A–D), then RAG indexing and recall (E–H).

runs.query: Fetch https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Claude_Shannon and tell me his birth date, death date, and three key contributions to information theory.
Find 3 family-friendly things to do in Tokyo this weekend. Check Saturday's weather forecast there and tell me which one is most appropriate.
My mom's birthday is 15 May 2026. Remember that and create reminders for two weeks before and on the day.

runs.run_hint: Queries durable memory and returns birthday.

runs.query: Search for 'Python asyncio best practices', read the top 3 results, and give me a short numbered list of the advice they agree on.

scenarios.label: E
scenarios.title: Index attention.md
scenarios.subtitle: Index one file, then answer from chunks.

runs.query: Index the file papers/attention.md and tell me what the three key contributions of the Transformer architecture are according to this paper.

scenarios.label: F
scenarios.title: Bulk index papers
scenarios.subtitle: Bulk index .md files; recall across runs.

runs.query: Index every .md file under papers/. Confirm how many chunks were indexed in total.

runs.run_hint: Fresh process; answer from persisted FAISS index.
runs.query: Across the papers I have indexed, what do they say about chain-of-thought reasoning?
Across these papers, how do they handle the credit assignment problem?
Compare how the ReAct paper and the Chain-of-Thought paper differ in their treatment of intermediate reasoning.
